title: Orobanche trichocalyx
taxonomy: Orobanche trichocalyx belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family. This family comprises a large number of species, including parasitic plants that obtain their nutrients by connecting to the roots of their host plants. Orobanche trichocalyx is specifically classified under the genus Orobanche, which includes over 200 species. The genus Orobanche is further divided into several sections, with O. trichocalyx belonging to the section Trichocaulis. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.

intro: Orobanche trichocalyx, commonly known as the rootless broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching to the roots of other plants, typically those in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Orobanche trichocalyx has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antihistamine, and immunomodulatory effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Orobanche trichocalyx is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used as a component in various blends, including those for respiratory issues and as an immunostimulant.
